---
title: "TODO — short imperative or noun phrase"
item-id: TODO-IS-NNN
type: issue
epic-id: TODO-epic-slug
initiative: TODO-initiative-slug
gantt-id: TODO
status: open                # open | in-progress | blocked | done
owner: TODO-owner           # current owner (advisory — see docs/)
priority: medium            # low | medium | high | critical
opened-date: TODO-YYYY-MM-DD
closed-date:                # populated when status: done
description: TODO — one-liner used in listing tables and the WU roll-up
related-items: []           # e.g. [A5-EX-002]
---

## Summary

> TODO — one paragraph. What the problem is, where it sits today, and (once
> closed) the final resolution. The advisor reads this first; everything below
> is for the next engineer (or future-me).

## Investigation

> TODO — technical narrative: findings, decisions, the data behind them.
> Reference media stored under this item's `media/` directory once it
> exists (placeholder paths stay as code, not links):
> `![](media/TODO-IS-NNN-IMG-evidence.png)`
>
> Cross-link related items with their IDs in prose, e.g.
> `[A1-EX-002](../../experiments/A1-EX-002-some-slug/README.qmd)`.

## Weekly summaries

<!-- One `### W<N> (<date>)` entry per week the issue moves. Newest on top.
     The `/disperse` skill appends these and copies the latest one into the
     weekly update's roll-up. Do not edit older entries — they are the
     advisor-visible history. -->

### W<N> (TODO-YYYY-MM-DD)

- Status: open
- TODO — one-bullet "what happened this week"

{{< include _files.md >}}

{{< include _revisions.md >}}
